description: Frankclaw feature verification registry. Source of truth for all custom features in the frankclaw fork. Used by verify-frankclaw-features.sh for automated three-probe verification.

features.name: Discord DNR quiet hours (replies)
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on Discord conversational replies via enforceDiscordDnrWindow

features.name: Discord DNR quiet hours (outbound)
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on Discord outbound messages from cron/sub-agents/raw_send

features.name: Discord DNR quiet hours (actions)
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on Discord action-based sends (sendMessage/threadCreate/threadReply). Helper lives in runtime.messaging.send-dnr.frankclaw.ts; call sites are in runtime.messaging.send.ts after the upstream split (commit 5201c42251).

static.note: Helper definition: isDiscordActionDnrSuppressed wraps enforceDiscordDnrWindow and returns true on DiscordDnrSuppressedError.
Must be called by sendMessage, threadCreate, and threadReply (3 call sites). Lower count means a code path bypasses DNR enforcement.

features.name: Discord DNR quiet hours (channel)
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on Discord channel-level outbound via outbound adapter

features.name: Bed reaction on DNR
features.description: Reacts with bed emoji when a message is suppressed by DNR quiet hours

features.name: Bed reaction on DNR (no-reply)
features.description: Reacts with bed emoji when agent produces no reply during DNR quiet hours, so sender knows the message was received but deferred

features.name: DNR deferred delivery
features.description: Enqueues then defers delivery when DNR suppresses a reply (enqueueDelivery before deferDelivery)

features.name: Durable inbound queue
features.description: Frankclaw durable worker for Discord inbound message processing with crash recovery, safe visibility timeout sizing, and coalesced batch handling

static.note: client must be passed so durable worker activates
Lease sizing helper that ensures durable queue visibility timeout is >= worker run timeout
Ensures durable queue batches are processed via coalesced handler when available

runtime.note: Startup-only probe. It fires on gateway start, so scheduled verification runs without a fresh restart should skip rather than fail. Post-restart health checks still validate it when a gateway boot actually occurs.
Only fires when Discord messages are queued and coalesced by the durable inbound queue

features.name: Message coalescing
features.description: Batches rapid-fire Discord messages into coalesced groups for efficient processing

features.name: Thread binding recovery
features.description: Recovers thread session bindings when preflight check fails, preventing orphaned threads

features.name: Gate-control unknown groups
features.description: WhatsApp group gating with frankclaw-specific mention keywords and gate modes

features.name: Raw send tool
features.description: Agent tool for direct cross-channel message delivery (raw_send)

features.name: Periodic delivery recovery
features.description: 2-minute interval recovery loop for failed/deferred deliveries

features.name: WhatsApp DNR quiet hours
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on WhatsApp outbound messages

features.name: Telegram DNR quiet hours
features.description: Enforces quiet hours on Telegram delivery replies and channel outbound

features.name: Discord autoThread
features.description: Auto-creates threads for all Discord replies when autoThread config is enabled. Implementation lives in threading.auto-thread.ts after the upstream threading split (commit 7e1acf2f1e); threading.ts is now a re-export barrel.

static.note: Implementation: must reference autoThread config flag (channelConfig?.autoThread), the autoThreadName option, and the autoThreadContext return value.

features.name: doramon_stop command
features.description: Typing doramon_stop in any channel aborts the current session run

features.name: DNR policy exports in plugin-sdk
features.description: Exports discord-dnr and delivery-queue modules via plugin-sdk for extensions to consume

features.name: Telegram gateMode
features.description: Per-chat gate mode support for Telegram groups (blocked/silent/open/frank-only/allowlist/mention)

features.name: Durable subagent spawn
features.description: Wraps subagent spawning with durable queue for crash recovery

features.name: Agent runner deferred retry
features.description: Detects retryable agent failures and implements deferred retry with backoff

features.name: Restart selfheal integration
features.description: Frankclaw restart self-heal logic integrated into the gateway CLI lifecycle, with a real end-to-end agent probe that requires the gateway to answer GATEWAY_OK instead of only appearing active

features.name: Cron self-heal
features.description: Frankclaw cron timer self-heal for stuck/failed jobs

features.name: Delivery queue (frankclaw)
features.description: Frankclaw delivery queue with enqueue-before-defer pattern for reliable deferred delivery

static.note: must be exported AND called in materializeDiscordInboundJob
called in toDiscordMessageEvent for batch path

features.name: SUBAGENTS.md global prepend
features.description: Loads SUBAGENTS.md from workspace and prepends its content to sub-agent spawn tasks and cron agentTurn prompts

static.note: import and call in spawn path
wraps childTaskMessage with global instructions
import and call in cron agentTurn path
wraps commandBody with global instructions for cron runs

runtime.note: Only fires on first sub-agent spawn after restart. Not a startup probe.

static.note: function defined AND called in recovery loop
deferUntilMs bypass in isEntryEligibleForRecoveryRetry + recovery DNR defer

features.name: PI embedded auth-aware stream wrapper
features.description: Preserves auth-profile API key injection for pi embedded runs via authStorage.getApiKey() credential resolution in resolveEmbeddedAgentStreamFn

static.note: authStorage parameter and usage in resolveEmbeddedAgentStreamFn for credential injection
auth-aware stream wrapper resolves apiKey via resolveEmbeddedAgentApiKey for provider
resolved wsApiKey injected into stream options for WebSocket transport

features.name: Matrix SDK Jiti singleton workaround
features.description: Prevents matrix-js-sdk singleton guard crashes when upstream plugin SDK loading plus Jiti create separate module graphs per plugin by externalizing all matrix-js-sdk subpaths and resetting __js_sdk_entrypoint before each plugin load

static.note: neverBundle must externalize the whole matrix-js-sdk package tree, not just the bare import
plugin loader uses shared Jiti loader cache to prevent Matrix SDK singleton guard crashes across plugin loads

features.name: Explicit exec capability baseline
features.description: Guards the frankclaw exec capability baseline by keeping schema and verification coverage for sandbox.mode=off and exec host=gateway, security=full, ask=off. Live ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json pinning is enforced operationally outside the repo, so repo verification must stay worktree-safe.

static.note: Schema test must keep sandbox.mode pinned to off as the baseline expectation
Schema test must keep exec.host pinned to gateway as the baseline expectation
Schema test must keep exec.security pinned to full as the baseline expectation
Schema test must keep exec.ask pinned to off as the baseline expectation

features.name: Channel config additionalProperties relaxation
features.description: Relaxes additionalProperties: false in upstream channel config schemas to allow frankclaw custom properties (gateMode, requireMention, autoThread, etc.)

features.name: sessions.send delivery context forwarding
features.description: When sessions.send forwards to chat.send, passes the target session stored delivery context as explicit originating route fields so replies route to the correct channel (e.g. Discord) instead of falling back to webchat.

runtime.note: Only fires when sessions.send delivers to a Discord session. Expected to be absent after cold restart with no cross-session sends.

features.name: Discord durable queue pre-process diagnostics
features.description: Logs session status before processing each Discord durable queue job to help diagnose silent message consumption.

runtime.note: Only fires when Discord messages arrive. Expected to be absent if no Discord messages received since restart.

features.name: WhatsApp group gating observability
features.description: Always-on info-level logging for group message gating decisions (skip/silent/process), offline catch-up filter drops, and group history buffer size. Enables diagnosing empty InboundHistory without verbose mode.

features.name: WhatsApp offline catch-up group history preservation
features.description: Store offline catch-up (append upsert) messages in groupHistories buffer for group context, even when too old for auto-reply. Fixes empty InboundHistory when messages arrive before gateway start.

features.name: Durable queue pre-run abort safety
features.description: Pre-run abort checks in processDiscordMessage throw instead of returning silently, preventing the durable queue from permanently deleting messages that were never processed

static.note: Three pre-run abort checks must throw instead of return to prevent message loss on gateway shutdown

features.name: Discord inbound lifecycle sinkhole recovery
features.description: Protects Discord inbound jobs from false-success sinkholes by requiring an explicit terminal lifecycle state, atomically materializing transcript files before session metadata persistence, and recovering stale pre-start lifecycle records on startup.

static.note: Durable worker must track explicit inbound lifecycle stages instead of treating handler return as success
Startup recovery must scan stale pre-start lifecycle records
Explicit hard error when a durable inbound claim/session-init never reaches run_started, reply_delivered, or dropped_intentionally

static.pattern: session metadata exists but transcript missing
static.note: Explicit hard error when session metadata materializes without a transcript file
Recovery helper must emit a dedicated warning for stale pre-start lifecycle records
Session file resolution must materialize the transcript before persisting session metadata
Frankclaw helper that creates the transcript file on disk before metadata persistence

runtime.logPattern: discord durable worker missing terminal inbound lifecycle state
runtime.note: Fires when the durable queue claimed and session-init path returned without run_started, reply_delivered, or dropped_intentionally

runtime.logPattern: discord durable worker session metadata exists but transcript missing
runtime.note: Fires when sessions.json materializes a session entry but the transcript file is still absent
Startup recovery found a leftover claimed/session_init/session_metadata_persisted/handler_returned record from an interrupted inbound run

runtime.logPattern: [frankclaw-durable-worker] session metadata exists but transcript missing:
runtime.note: Recovery scan found persisted session metadata whose transcript file is absent, even if no transcript evidence exists

features.name: Memory search path prepend
features.description: Prepends file path (e.g. [knowledge/family/charlotte.md]) to each chunk text before embedding and FTS indexing, improving search recall for path-scoped queries

static.note: Assignment prepending path to chunk text must exist in both embedding and FTS-only code paths
Comment markers for both code paths (embedding + FTS-only)

features.name: Subagent announce: direct delivery must be user-visible
features.description: Direct subagent completion handoff only counts as delivered when the parent produces a user-facing reply, enabling thread completion fallback when no visible payload is detected.

features.name: Subagent announce: thread completion fallback
features.description: When the agent gateway method returns no visible payload, extract completion text from internal events and send it directly to the origin thread via sendCompletionFallback. Replaces the earlier session-store reply fallback approach.

runtime.note: Emitted when the thread completion fallback fires

features.name: System event suppression for heartbeat and exec completions
features.description: Suppresses internal heartbeat and exec completion system-event noise from user-facing prompts, preventing leakage into live Discord threads and other sessions.

features.name: Subagent announce dead-letter alert
features.description: When subagent completion announce retries are exhausted or expire, emit a dead-letter warning to Discord logs so missing completions are visible.

features.name: ACPX resume prefers agentSessionId over stale acpxSessionId
features.description: Dead-session ACP repair and persisted ACP resume prefer agentSessionId so Claude transcript continuity follows the live underlying agent session instead of the stale ACPX queue-owner id.

features.name: Discord ACP thread binding prefix strip
features.description: Strips channel:/user: prefix from conversation IDs before Discord API calls in the session binding adapter's child-placement bind path. Without this, the plugin refactor's resolveInboundConversation returns prefixed IDs that Discord rejects.

features.name: Discord auto-thread durable worker session key tracking
features.description: Durable worker tracks progress against the resolved session key (from onReplyPlanResolved) when auto-thread creation moves the session to a new channel key, preventing dead-letters of messages that triggered thread creation (2026-04-09 regression).

features.name: Subagent failure reply instruction forces user-visible LLM response
features.description: For subagent task outcomes with status 'error', 'timeout', or 'unknown', the replyInstruction baked into the parent agent's internal task_completion event explicitly forbids NO_REPLY and mandates a user-facing summary of the failure plus a next step. Prior behavior allowed NO_REPLY when the LLM thought content had already been delivered; combined with the earlier scope bug in the direct-fallback path (commit 49781f2d00), this silently dropped every failure notification reaching the user. Successful outcomes (status 'ok') retain the NO_REPLY escape hatch so in-flight progress updates don't spam the channel.

features.name: Subagent completion announce direct fallback delivery
features.description: When a subagent completion announce reaches the parent session but the parent produces no visible gateway payload, fall back to sending the completion text directly to the origin thread via sendMessage. Uses sendCompletionFallback with sendMessage dep.

features.name: ACP manager registers subagent run for announce delivery
features.description: The ACP manager now also registers a subagent run (registerSubagentRun) when creating a background task record. Previously, ACP workers spawned via the gateway agent method (not sessions_spawn) only created a task registry record. The announce/delivery flow reads the separate subagent registry, so without this registration, results from ACP workers were silently dropped — no announce was ever attempted.

static.note: Import + usage in createBackgroundTaskRecord

features.name: ACP progress summary keeps tail not head
features.description: The ACP background task progress summary now keeps the TAIL of the text instead of the HEAD when truncating to 240 chars. The terminal-result check (resolveBackgroundTaskTerminalResult) looks for patterns like 'now let me' to detect workers that stopped at a progress checkpoint. With head-truncation, early narration from a worker that completed 168 tool calls and created a PR would match, causing false-positive 'blocked' verdicts. Tail-truncation means the check sees how the run ended.

static.note: Comment explaining why tail truncation matters

features.name: ACP background task terminal safety
features.description: Promotes ACP background-task false-greens into explicit blocked or lost states. Progress-checkpoint endings are marked blocked, and stale ACP tasks are reconciled lost when the session store read fails after grace.

static.pattern: ACP run stopped at a progress checkpoint instead of a terminal result.
do NOT assume the session is alive

features.name: Subagent completion fallback sanitizes internal context
features.description: The fallback delivery path extracts user-facing text from structured internal events via extractThreadCompletionFallbackText, avoiding raw internal context leakage. Sanitization of internal runtime context is centralized in deliver.ts.

static.note: Definition + usage in fallback path
Centralized guard against sending fenced content to external channels

features.name: GPT-5 VOICE.md writing style injection
features.description: Loads VOICE.md from workspace at runtime and appends it as a Writing Style section to the GPT-5 output contract. Falls back gracefully when VOICE.md is absent.

static.note: VOICE.md filename constant
Function definition + usage in resolveOpenAISystemPromptContribution
Writing Style section header appended when VOICE.md present

features.name: Durable queue startup reclaim
features.description: On gateway startup, immediately reclaims all in-flight durable queue jobs from the previous process instead of waiting for lease expiry

static.note: Function definition + startup call

features.name: verify-acp-roundtrip.sh node resolution guard
features.description: The post-merge ACP smoke test script picks a supported Node (>=22.12) explicitly and prepends it to PATH (stripping nvm node dirs that would shadow it) before invoking `openclaw`, so callers with an nvm default pointing at an unsupported Node cannot make `/usr/bin/openclaw` hit its `Node.js v22.12+ is required` guard and silently misreport a CLI startup failure as an ACP dispatch regression. Also runs an explicit `openclaw --version` probe so CLI startup failures produce a distinct FAIL message.

static.pattern: FAIL: openclaw CLI failed to start
FAIL: no Node >=22.12 found on host

features.name: Subagent announce parent rewake
features.description: After deliverSubagentAnnouncement on a completion, request a heartbeat wake on the parent session so the parent agent processes the announce as a turn (commit/push/PR/reply). On delivery failure, write a retry marker that openclaw-watchdog announce-retry drains and nudges via openclaw sessions send.

features.name: Discord thread starter injection disabled
features.description: Frankclaw disables upstream's Discord thread-starter context injection. Upstream fetches the parent-channel message that created a Discord thread and injects it as `[Thread starter - for context]` on every inbound turn. For long-running threads this becomes a stale anchor — e.g. a thread kicked off days ago with `start that F28B v4 training pass` kept anchoring the LLM on F28B+v4 even after 5 days of iteration had moved to v6/v10. Session history already carries recent state; the starter body just fought recency signals and misled the agent on ambiguous prompts. We leave `threadStarterBody` declared (undefined) so downstream template gates keep compiling, but we neither call Discord REST nor populate the field. The upstream `channelConfig.includeThreadStarter` knob is intentionally ignored — frankclaw policy is OFF, full stop. Root incident: 2026-04-18 Discord thread 1493276394732519577 (`F28B Integration v4 Metric Deltas`). After the Apr 29 upstream kernel-turn refactor (commit 9a9cd0c0ab) the includeThreadStarter logic moved from message-handler.process.ts to message-handler.context.ts.

features.name: Scoped prompt injections
features.description: Per-channel/thread/group prompt injection registry. Operators register short XML-tagged instruction blocks that get prepended to every inbound turn matching a scope. Matchers: exact channel type, exact session key, session-key regex, or Discord thread-name regex. Registry lives at state/channel-prompt-injections.json (survives upstream merges). Managed via scripts/scoped-prompt.sh (list|add|rm|show|enable|disable|test) and the scoped-prompt-injections skill. Inbound injection is a single hook in src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-run.ts calling resolveScopedPromptForContext from src/auto-reply/scoped-prompt.frankclaw.ts. Outbound enforcement for cross-channel delivery is implemented in src/auto-reply/outbound-scoped-prompt.frankclaw.ts and is applied to cron isolated delivery prompts plus message-tool/raw_send cross-session preflight.

features.name: Doramon note-to-self protocol
features.description: Canonical `[Doramon note to self]` prefix (+ legacy `[doramon you forgot to answer!]:` back-compat) for bot-authored messages that should trigger an agent turn. Producers (task-registry Background task announces, deadletter-recover) wrap their output via `wrapAsNoteToSelf()`. The Discord inbound preflight lets these through the bot-self-drop gate. The reply pipeline injects `noteToSelfPromptOverlay()` when the prefix matches, enforcing the Frank directive: ALWAYS respond with a user-visible summary; iterate if actions remain; otherwise summarize what was completed. Never reply NO_REPLY to a note-to-self. Replaces the ad-hoc `[doramon you forgot to answer!]:` prefix used previously only by deadletter-recover.

features.name: Direct-action DNR bypass
features.description: Allows agent sends marked directAction=true to bypass quiet hours DNR enforcement (for fulfilling explicit user requests during quiet hours). Uses AsyncLocalStorage context, logs every bypass.

features.name: Note-to-self pre-debounce bypass
features.description: Bot-self messages with [Doramon note to self] or legacy [doramon you forgot to answer!]: prefix bypass the pre-debounce filter in Discord message-handler.ts and reach preflight/relay

workspaceChecks.name: CC context injection symlinks
workspaceChecks.description: AGENTS.md, MEMORY.md, SOUL.md, and CLAUDE.md symlinks in ~/.claude/ point to workspace originals so Claude Code auto-injects OpenClaw context into every session

workspaceChecks.name: Exec approval always-allow
workspaceChecks.description: exec-approvals.json must have defaults.askFallback set to full so exec can proceed even if approval prompting is unavailable
workspaceChecks.fix: python3 -c "import json; f='~/.openclaw/exec-approvals.json'; import os; p=os.path.expanduser(f); d=json.load(open(p)); d.setdefault('defaults',{})['askFallback']='full'; json.dump(d,open(p,'w'),indent=2)"

workspaceChecks.name: Exec security settings
workspaceChecks.description: openclaw.json must have tools.exec.ask=off and agents.defaults.sandbox.mode=off so execution is never gated

workspaceChecks.name: SKILL.md Discord slash commands
workspaceChecks.description: Auto-registers commands from skills/commands/SKILL.md as Discord slash commands so /iterate etc appear in Discord autocomplete

runtime.logPattern: registered .* skill commands from SKILL.md as slash commands
runtime.note: Fires on gateway startup when SKILL.md has commands

workspaceChecks.name: Terminated error as timeout failover
workspaceChecks.description: Classifies bare 'terminated' errors (from lane/session aborts) as timeout so the fallback chain continues to cross-provider candidates instead of short-circuiting

workspaceChecks.name: Provider circuit breaker for cross-provider fallback
workspaceChecks.description: Process-level circuit breaker that tracks consecutive timeout failures per provider and skips tripped providers in the fallback chain, enabling self-healing cross-provider fallback (e.g. OpenAI → Gemini) without gateway restart

runtime.note: Fires when a provider hits 3 consecutive timeouts
Fires when a tripped provider responds successfully

workspaceChecks.name: Fallback chain never aborts on unknown errors
workspaceChecks.description: Removes the early throw on unrecognized errors at the last fallback candidate, ensuring all candidates (including cross-provider like Gemini) are always tried before giving up

static.pattern: never abort the fallback loop on unrecognized errors

workspaceChecks.name: Doramon todo-loop driver rewake
workspaceChecks.description: Workspace-side shell driver that claims pending doramon-todo-loop todos atomically and rewakes the origin session via 'openclaw system event --mode next-heartbeat' so the main agent picks up the todo on its next heartbeat turn under the audit-first contract from skills/doramon-todo-loop/SKILL.md. Replaces the Discord-note and cron-one-shot dispatch paths that were unreliable. Driver fires every 2 minutes via the openclaw-watchdog todo-loop-driver routine.

runtime.note: Fires every 2 minutes from openclaw-watchdog; presence confirms driver routine is wired.

workspaceChecks.name: Doramon todo-loop cron-tick dispatch
workspaceChecks.description: Driver dispatches pending todos via 'openclaw cron run <tick-id>' — a proven-reliable primitive that enqueues an isolated agent turn through the gateway's cron scheduler. The tick cron job (see cron/doramon-todo-loop-tick.md) reads the pending queue, picks highest priority, and spawns a Claude Code ACP worker per the audit-first contract. Replaces earlier broken paths: Discord-note indirection, --at cron one-shots that never fired, and --mode now system events that enqueued but didn't drive turns.

workspaceChecks.name: ACP concurrency limit: evict idle sessions at limit
workspaceChecks.description: When maxConcurrentSessions is reached, evict cached sessions with no active turn before rejecting. Prevents deadlock where finished-but-cached sessions fill all slots.

workspaceChecks.name: Fatal plugin load errors (no silent failures)
workspaceChecks.description: Gateway crashes on plugin load failures instead of silently continuing without broken plugins. Prevents masked bugs like zod v3/v4 mismatches killing channels silently.
